summary: Russian forces are deploying the "Yolki" and "Yolka" interceptor drones—autonomous, AI-equipped systems weighing 1.3 kg, reaching speeds of 230 km/h, and capable of engaging targets up to 3 km away—to counter Ukrainian FPV and fixed-wing UAVs. The "Rubikon" Center reported destroying 100 Ukrainian targets on March 30, including personnel, fixed-wing UAVs, and control stations, in joint operations with the Russian Air Force. Ukrainian forces used electronic warfare to force FPV drone landings and survived a Russian FPV attack near Kostiantynivka, aided by drone detectors. Russian Ka-52 crews were eliminated after a crash landing near Nadiivka, with additional drones striking during evacuation. Russia is also advancing its "Рассвет" low-orbit satellite network, aiming to rival Starlink with 250 satellites by 2027 and 730 by 2030.
